title: Works At vs Mr. Anuj Jain on 26 March, 2012
body: Works At vs Mr. Anuj Jain on 26 March, 2012 IN THE COURT OF MS. NEELAM SINGH, ADDL. DISTRICT JUDGE, SOUTH-II, SAKET, NEW DELHI. TM No. 79/11 Balajee Manufacturing Co. Through its Sole Prop. Sh. Kamal Kumar Mundhra Having office at : 109 Basement, Vinoba Puri, Lajpat Nagar-II, New Delhi. Branch office at: 3162, Kanti Nagar, Delhi-110051 Works at: 15/07 Site-IV Industrial Area, Sahibabad, Ghaziabad. ...Plaintiff Versus 1. Mr. Anuj Jain Prop/ Director/ Partner M/s. Sun Poly Bag Pvt. Ltd. 257 FIE, Patpar Ganj Industrial Area, Delhi. 2. Mr. Gulshan Arora Prop/ Director/ Partner/ Authorised Signatory M/s. Arora Printing and cutting 26/2 Gali No. 7, Raghubar Pura, Gandhi Nagar, Delhi-31. 3. Mr. Sanjiv Goswami Prop/ Director/ Partner/ Authorised Signatory 1455/16 Ground Floor, Sri Ram Market, Gali Chitiyan - Maliwara, Nai Sarak, Delhi-6. T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 1/8 4. Other unknown associates of the above defendants ...Defendants PLAINT PRESENTED ON : 28.06.2010 ARGUMENT HEARD ON : 26.03.2011 JUDGMENT ON : 26.03.2011 JUDGMENT 1. Vide this order, I shall dispose off the application filed on behalf of defendant under order 7 rule 11 read with section 151 CPC for rejection of plaint on the ground that the present suit does not disclose any cause of action against the defendants and also on the ground that as per the provisions of Section 27 of the Trademark Act, 1999 the suit is not maintainable. 2. It is submitted by Ld. counsel for defendant that the plaintiff was not the registered proprietor of the impugned trademark "Balajee" at the time of filing of the present suit before this court. 3. During the arguments, it is argued by Ld. counsel for the defendant that even this court has no territorial jurisdiction to entertain the present suit. It is submitted by Ld. counsel for defendant that in para 18 of the plaint, the jurisdiction of this court has been invoked on the account of fact that the plaintiff is having its office in Delhi and works for gain in Delhi within the T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 2/8 territorial jurisdiction of this court. It is further submitted that the plaint is completely silent as to the fact whether the defendants are residing, carrying on business or personally works for gain within the jurisdiction of this court. 4. It is further submitted that although one more ground of jurisdiction has been provided by the plaintiff under section 134 of Trademark Act, 1999 but as per section 134 (2) only suits which falls in clause (A) & (B) of sub clause (1) then suits for infringement of registered trademark or suits relating to any right in a registered trademark can be instituted in a district court. 5. It is further submitted that if the suits are falling in clause (c) of section 134 sub-clause (1) then the same can be instituted only as per the provision of section 20 CPC. It is further submitted by Ld. counsel for defendant that even if it is presumed that plaintiff is carrying on its business at 109, Basement, Vinoba Puri, Lajpat Nagar-II, New Delhi-110024 but not even a single document has been placed on record by the plaintiff to prove its address. It is further submitted that even this address of Lajpat Nagar never belonged to the plaintiff and just belong to one lawyer as per the report of the process server and postal authorities. T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 3/8 6. It is accepted by the plaintiff that no document pertaining to address of Lajpat Nagar on the basis of which jurisdiction of this court has been invoked is placed on record by them. An application under order 7 rule 10 A(1) (2) CPC filed for seeking the directions to send the plaint of the present suit to the court of District Judge, East, Karkardooma, Delhi. 7. Arguments heard. Record perused carefully. 8. Upon facts and circumstances of this case, I am of this considered opinion that court is to only proceed with the matter after being prima facie satisfied about its territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the suit. In the cases of: ( a) Dhodha House vs. S.K. Maingi , (2006) 9 SCC 4; ( b) Dabour India Ltd. vs. K.R. Industries , (2006) 33 PTC 348; ( c) Archie Comic Publications vs. Purple Creations Pvt. Ltd. , 172 (2010) DLT 234; Wherein Hon'ble Supreme Court has held that: "It is trite law that a judgment and order passed by the court having no territorial jurisdiction would be nullity." 9. As per the provisions of Section 134 sub-clause 2 an additional ground to file suit has been provided to the plaintiff apart from T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 4/8 Section 20 CPC. S.134 . Suit for infringement etc., to be instituted before District Court. - (1) No Suit - (a) for the infringement of a registered trade mark; or (b) relating to any right in a registered trade mark; or (c) for passing off arising out of the use by the defendant of any trade mark which is identical with or deceptively similar to the plaintiff's trade mark, whether registered or unregistered, shall be instituted in any court inferior to a District Court having jurisdiction to try the suit. (2) For the purpose of clauses (a) and (b) of sub-section (1), a "District Court having jurisdiction" shall, notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Civil Procedure 1908 (5 of 1908) or any other law for the time being in force, include a District Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction, at the time of the institution of the suit or other proceedings, the person instituting the suit or proceeding, or, where there are more than one such persons any of them, actually and voluntarily resides or carries on business or personally works for gain. Explanation - For the purposes of sub-section (2), "person" includes the registered proprietor and the registered user. 20. Other suits to be instituted where defendants reside or cause of action arises. - T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 5/8 subject to the limitations aforesaid, every suit shall be instituted in a Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction- (a) the defendant, or each of the defendants where there are more than one, at the time of commencement of the suit, actually and voluntarily resides, or carries on business or personally works for gain; or (b) any of the defendants, where there are more than one, at the time of the commencement of the suit, actually and voluntarily resides, or carries on business, or personally works for gain, provided that in such case either the leave of the Court is given, or the defendants who do not reside, or carry on business, or personally work for gain, as aforesaid, acquiesce in such institution; or (c) the cause of action, wholly or in part, arises. [Explanation].- A corporation shall be deemed to carry on business at its sole or principal office in [India] or, in respect of any cause of action arising at any place where it has also a subordinate office, at such place. 10.In terms of the decision in Dhodha House v. S.K. Maingi , (2006) 9 SCC 41, it is held that a corporation can be said to be carrying on its business only through its principal office or through its subsidiary offices and even exclusive dealers at a certain place would not enable it to claim that it is carrying on business at that place. In fact, there is not even an averment that T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 6/8 the alleged dealer is an exclusive dealer, even though the defendant reiterates that even an exclusive dealership would not qualify for the purposes of Section 134 of the 1999 Act. Further a company incorporated under the Companies Act is supposed to maintain records and act only in accordance with its Memorandum and Articles which again ought to be in accordance with the Companies Act . In such circumstances, any averment of the plaintiff, as a company, about itself, ought to be supported by documents, which is not the situation in the present case. The plaintiff has and ought to have the documents in its possession and they ought to be presented with the plaint itself so as to save the suit from return under Order 7 Rule 10 CPC . 11.Keeping in view the above discussions, the suit of the plaintiff is returned to be presented before the Court of competent jurisdiction. Interim order dated 28.06.2010 against the defendant shall continue for 7 days from the date of this order and thereafter interim order shall stands vacated. 12.Plaint be returned to the plaintiff as per law. File be consigned to record room after necessary compliance. (NEELAM SINGH) ADJ-02, SOUTH, T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 7/8 SAKET, NEW DELHI 26.03.2012/ MC T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 8/8 Balajee Manufacturing Co. vs. Anuj Jain & Ors. TM No. 79/11 26.03.2012 Present: Counsel for the plaintiff Counsel for the defendant. Vide separate order, the suit of the plaintiff is returned to be presented before the Court of competent jurisdiction. Interim order dated 28.06.2010 against the defendant shall continue for 7 days from the date of this order and thereafter interim order shall stands vacated. Ahlmad is directed to return the plaint as per law. File be consigned to record room after necessary compliance. (NEELAM SINGH) ADJ-02, SOUTH, SAKET, NEW DELHI 26.03.2012 T.M. No. 79/11 Page no. 9/8
